theme: Creation, early humanity, and the beginnings of God's covenant people.
God delivers Israel from slavery and gives His covenant law.
Holiness, worship, and the laws for Israel's covenant life.
Israel's wilderness journey and God's faithfulness despite rebellion.
Moses renews the covenant and calls Israel to obey God.
Israel enters and settles the promised land under God's leadership.
Israel's repeated rebellion and God's deliverance through judges.
Faithfulness, redemption, and God's providence through ordinary lives.
Israel transitions from judges to kings under Saul and David.
David's reign shows both God's covenant promise and human failure.
Israel's kingdom divides as kings rise, fall, and turn from God.
Israel and Judah decline into exile because of persistent disobedience.
Israel's history is retold with focus on David, worship, and covenant.
Judah's kings, temple worship, and the need for covenant faithfulness.
God restores His people from exile and rebuilds temple worship.
Jerusalem's walls are rebuilt as the people renew their covenant.
God preserves His people through courage and hidden providence.
Suffering, faith, and God's wisdom beyond human understanding.
Prayers and songs of worship, lament, trust, and praise.
Wisdom for faithful living rooted in the fear of the Lord.
Life's limits and meaning are understood through reverence for God.
Love, desire, and the beauty of covenant relationship.
Judgment, hope, and God's promised salvation through His servant.
Judgment on Judah and the promise of a new covenant.
Grief over Jerusalem's fall and hope in God's mercy.
God's glory, judgment, and promise to restore His people.
Faithfulness in exile and God's rule over kingdoms.
God's faithful love toward an unfaithful people.
The day of the Lord calls for repentance and promises restoration.
God demands justice, righteousness, and true worship.
God judges pride and promises deliverance for His people.
God's mercy reaches even those people consider enemies.
God requires justice, mercy, and humble faithfulness.
God judges oppressive nations and defends His people.
Faith trusts God's justice even when His ways are hard to understand.
The day of the Lord brings judgment and future restoration.
God calls His restored people to rebuild the temple.
God encourages His people with visions of restoration and the coming King.
God confronts covenant unfaithfulness and points to future renewal.
Jesus is the promised Messiah and King.
Jesus is the suffering Son of God who serves and saves.
Jesus brings salvation to all people.
Jesus is the Son of God who gives eternal life.
The Holy Spirit empowers the church to spread the gospel.
God's righteousness is revealed through faith in Jesus Christ.
Paul corrects the church and teaches faithful life in Christ.
Paul defends his ministry and teaches strength through weakness.
Believers are justified by faith and called to freedom in Christ.
The church is united in Christ and called to holy living.
Joy and faithful endurance come through life centered on Christ.
Christ is supreme over creation, the church, and Christian life.
Paul encourages faithfulness, holiness, and hope in Christ's return.
Paul clarifies Christ's return and encourages steadfast living.
Paul instructs Timothy on church leadership and sound teaching.
Paul urges endurance, faithfulness, and confidence in Scripture.
Paul teaches church order, sound doctrine, and godly living.
Paul appeals for forgiveness and reconciliation in Christ.
Jesus is the superior high priest and fulfillment of God's promises.
True faith is shown through obedience, wisdom, and works.
Believers endure suffering with hope and holy conduct.
Peter warns against false teachers and calls believers to grow in grace.
True fellowship with God is marked by love, truth, and obedience.
Believers are called to walk in truth and love.
Faithful believers support truth and practice godly hospitality.
Believers must contend for the faith against false teaching.
Christ triumphs, evil is judged, and God's kingdom is fully restored.
